fact: pnpm v10 requires `pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies` array in root package.json to allow native package build scripts (e.g. @prisma/client, @prisma/engines, bcrypt, prisma). The .npmrc `allow-build` key does NOT work in pnpm v10.
why: pnpm v10 introduced Safe Mode — build scripts are blocked by default. The only supported config path is package.json#pnpm.onlyBuiltDependencies, not .npmrc.

fact: Use `prisma migrate dev --create-only --name <name>` to generate migration SQL without applying it. This is required when you need to edit the SQL (e.g. add tsvector triggers, CHECK constraints, GIN indexes) before first application. Plain `prisma migrate dev --name <name>` applies immediately — editing the SQL afterward is a no-op because the migration is already recorded in _prisma_migrations.
why: Prisma records migrations as applied when they execute. --create-only defers execution so custom SQL edits take effect on first apply.

fact: When mocking @prisma/client for a service that `extends PrismaClient`, use a class stub (not jest.fn().mockImplementation). The `extends` keyword uses the prototype chain — methods like $connect/$disconnect must exist on the prototype, not on the constructor return value. Also required when prisma generate has not run yet (schema has no models).
why: jest.fn().mockImplementation returns a plain object; prototype methods from `extends` still resolve from PrismaClient.prototype, which is undefined if mocked with jest.fn(). A class stub sets up the prototype correctly.

fact: After applying a migration containing Unsupported('tsvector') columns, `prisma migrate dev` detects schema drift and prompts for a new migration name. This is expected behavior — the original migration was applied successfully. The prompt is Prisma's attempt to reconcile the Unsupported type it cannot manage. Safe to cancel (Ctrl-C) or ignore.
why: Prisma cannot introspect/manage native PostgreSQL types marked as Unsupported. It sees a diff between its internal model and the live DB schema even when they match.

fact: Place integration tests in `backend/test/` (not `backend/src/`) with a separate `backend/test/jest-e2e.json` config. The unit test runner uses jest.config.js with rootDir: 'src' — any *.spec.ts in src/ is discovered and run without a live DB, causing failures.
why: jest rootDir: 'src' scopes test discovery to src/. Integration tests need a live PostgreSQL (DATABASE_URL) so they must be isolated from the unit runner. The jest-e2e.json sets rootDir: '../' and testRegex: '.integration.spec.ts$'.
